summary: Gilead Sciences Inc. said it now expects to book a loss in 2026 on $11.5 billion in charges after deals to acquire a string of companies making experimental cancer and autoimmune disease drugs.

title: Musk Risks French Charges Over X Violations After Paris Snub
summary: Paris prosecutors are seeking charges against Elon Musk after he snubbed a summons to testify in April over how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content on social media platform X.

title: Oil Climbs Following Fresh Clashes Between US and Iranian Forces
summary: Oil jumped following renewed clashes between US and Iranian forces, jeopardizing the outlook for a deal to end the 10-week war.

title: US Stock Futures Fall, Oil Climbs on Iran Tensions: Markets Wrap
summary: US stock-index futures edged down and crude oil rose as escalating tensions in the Middle East revived concerns over energy supplies and tested the durability of a record-breaking stocks rally.

title: Gold Steady as US-Iran Clashes Dim Truce Prospects in Mideast
summary: Gold was steady as optimism that a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz was imminent faded following reports of attacks on US Navy vessels, reviving inflation concerns.

title: Foreign Central Banks Boost Share of Malaysian Bonds to Record
summary: Foreign central banks and governments have raised their share of Malaysian sovereign bonds to a record, underscoring the nation’s growing appeal as a reserve asset in a volatile global environment.

title: Veja os Três Assuntos Que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
summary:
O repórter Daniel Carvalho conta quais foram os três assuntos que 
marcaram a semana no Brasil. Com produção de Barbara Nascimento e edição de Raphael Almeida (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Macquarie Profit Jumps, Commodities Chief Paid $25.5 Million
summary: Macquarie Group Ltd.’s commodities and global markets division chief Simon Wright got a pay bump after profit within the bank’s key business drove earnings growth at the Australian firm.

title: Texas Roadhouse Sales Hold as Diners Pick Cheaper Beef Cuts
summary: Texas Roadhouse Inc. shares climbed after the restaurant operator posted in-line first‑quarter results and signaled that sales momentum would continue as diners adjusted to higher prices and commodity-cost inflation moderates slightly.

title: Compass Breaks 5-Year Brazil IPO Drought With $650 Million Offer
summary: Compass Gas e Energia SA’s shareholders raised 3.2 billion reais ($650 million) the first initial public offering in Brazil in almost five years, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Fed’s Collins Agreed With FOMC Dissenters Over Statement
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins said she agreed with colleagues who dissented at last week’s monetary policy gathering over wording in the Fed’s post-meeting statement that suggested the central bank would eventually resume rate reductions.

title: Nuevo presidente de Honduras evalúa los lazos con China
summary: Nasry Asfura, el presidente recién investido de Honduras, está revisando los acuerdos firmados con China por su predecesora, Xiomara Castro, antes de tomar una decisión sobre el restablecimiento de las relaciones con Taiwán. Asfura conversó con Erik Schatzker de Bloomberg el 6 de mayo, en el marco de la Conferencia Global del Milken Institute en Beverly Hills.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BASF Coatings’ €3.9 Billion Debt Deal Lures Threefold Demand
summary: A debt package for the auto paint and finishing division of European chemicals firm BASF SE drew demand of over three times the deal’s size, as investor interest in the troubled sector starts to revive after months in the doldrums.

title: Gundlach Warns About the Risks Facing Private Credit
summary: Jeffrey Gundlach, CEO and CIO of DoubleLine, discusses the current state of the private credit market. Gundlach compares the present moment in private credit to the conditions seen in 2007, highlighting significant risks and potential domino effects. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: $10 Billion Golub Fund Caps Outflows After Requests for 8.5%
summary: Golub Capital is capping withdrawals from its private credit fund after investors sought to pull 8.5% of shares, the latest instance of a money manager restricting outflows amid a wave of redemption requests.

title: Blackstone-Led Group Set to Inject $100 Million Into Medallia
summary: A group of private credit investors led by Blackstone Inc. is preparing to inject at least $100 million of fresh funds into Medallia Inc. as part of a restructuring that will put them in control of the software maker, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: MIT Media Lab Working To Advance Wearable Tech
summary: Dr. Pattie Maes, Head of Fluid Interfaces Group at MIT Media Lab joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the evolution of health tools and how the devices we already live with can help us become better versions of ourselves. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wynn Expects ‘Modest Delay’ in Opening of New Middle East Casino
summary: Wynn Resorts Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Craig Billings said the company now expects a “modest delay” in the opening date of a new casino resort it’s building in the United Arab Emirates.

title: Oil Swings on Fading Optimism for Resolution on Iran Conflict
summary: Oil prices whipsawed Thursday on renewed doubts about a deal to end the US war with Iran and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: Flex Announces Spin-Off of Cloud, Power Infrastructure Unit
summary:
Revathi Advaithi, CEO of Flex joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their earnings, health of the labor market, and what the company is doing to help hyper-scalers build data center capacity needs.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Coinbase Swings to a Loss While Posting Another Revenue Drop
summary: Just days after announcing deep job cuts and an artificial-intelligence push, Coinbase Global Inc. showed how tightly its fortunes remain tied to crypto markets.

title: Elanco Animal Health Boosts Full Year Forecast
summary: Elanco Animal Health boosted its adjusted earnings per share forecast for the full year. Jeff Simmons, President and CEO at Elanco Animal Health joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their company earnings and how the pet care market is doing.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Private Credit’s Birthplace Gives New Rise to 1980s-Style Greed
summary: For the past several years, private credit shone bright as the latest form of the “junk” debt revolution — one that began in the 1980s with Michael Milken’s Drexel Burnham Lambert and spawned a broader universe of leveraged lending.

title: Doubleline's Shinoda Sees Good Times Ahead for Commercial Real Estate
summary: Ken Shinoda, Doubleline non-agency residential mortgage-backed securities head, talks about the future of the housing market and mortgages. He says there are good times ahead for commercial real estate.  He speaks on a special edition of "Bloomberg The Close" from Los Angeles. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fewer Private Credit Managers After Volatility: Lam
summary: Gretchen Lam, CEO of Octagon Credit Investors, and Danielle Poli, co-portfolio manager, global credit at Oaktree Capital, join Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bolivia Sells $1 Billion in Bonds After Four-Year Break
summary: Bolivia sold dollar bonds on international capital markets for the first time in four years, as its new market-friendly government seizes on a tightening sovereign spread to raise fresh financing.

title: Gundlach Compares the Private Credit Market to the Wild West
summary: DoubleLine CEO and CIO Jeffrey Gundlach expresses his concerns about the state of the private credit market on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Gains for Third Day as Traders Weigh US-Iran Ceasefire Deal
summary: Gold held on to meager gains Thursday as traders assessed the prospects of a US-Iran truce deal.

title: DraftKings Profit Beats Estimates as Sportsbook Sales Grow
summary: DraftKings Inc. reported revenue that rose 17% to $1.65 billion, slightly ahead of analysts’ estimates for $1.63 billion.

title: Blue Owl Says It Will Be More Selective Making Software Loans
summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. will be more cautious lending to software companies and has begun to reduce its exposure to the sector, executives told investors in two of its private credit funds.
